zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Agnes of Bengeworth, Worcestershire Weeks於1505年1月1日出生在英國武斯特斯爾本沃斯時，她的父親John James Weeks32歲，母親Lady Alice de Bates31歲。她於1517年與Thomas Watson在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾本沃斯結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1576年3月在英國瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃享年71歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Agnes of Bengeworth, Worcestershire Weeks于1505年1月1日出生在英国武斯特斯爾本沃斯时，她的父亲John James Weeks32岁，母亲Lady Alice de Bates31岁。她于1517年与Thomas Watson在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾本沃斯结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1576年3月在英国瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃享年71岁，埋葬在英国瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃。

nameMeanings.name: Weeks
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Norwegian and Swedish   Vik  .

History: This surname was brought to North America independently by several different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One of the earliest on record is Leonard Weeks, who emigrated from Somerset, England, to Portsmouth, NH, some time before 1656. — William Weeks, recorded in Edgartown, Martha's Vineyard, MA, from 1653 onward, was probably a cousin or other relative of the John Wickes mentioned at   Wickes  .

variants.name: Wicks
meanings: English (southern England and East Anglia): generally a variant of Wick , Wyke , or the obsolete form Week, with post-medieval excrescent -s. Occasionally from the plural form of Middle English wik(e), late Middle English weke ‘building, especially one where dairy products are made’ (compare Wick ). An alternative Middle English plural form occurs in Wicken .

variants.name: Weekes
meanings: English: variant of   Weeks   or   Wicks  . This form of the surname is frequent in Barbados.

variants.name: Vik
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Alf, Erik, Lars, Lennart, Nels, Nils.

Norwegian and Swedish: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads all over Norway and Sweden, so named with Old Norse vík ‘small bay, inlet’. In Swedish it may also be a topographic or ornamental name. Compare Weeks .
Czech: from a short form of the personal name Viktor (see Victor ).

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Bengeworth,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Agnes of Bengeworth, Worcestershire Weeks was born on 1 January 1505, in Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England, her father, John James Weeks, was 32 and her mother, Lady Alice de Bates, was 31. She married Thomas Watson in 1517, in Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 7 daughters. She died in March 1576, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England, at the age of 71, and was buried in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Agnes of Bengeworth, Worcestershire Weeks est née le 1 janvier 1505, à Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, John James Weeks, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Lady Alice de Bates, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Watson en 1517, à Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée en mars 1576, à Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterrée à Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Agnes of Bengeworth, Worcestershire Weeks am 1. Januar 1505 in Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John James Weeks 32 und ihre Mutter Lady Alice de Bates 31. Sie hat 1517 in Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom Thomas Watson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 10 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb im März 1576 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England und wurde in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Agnes of Bengeworth, Worcestershire Weeks è nata il il 1 gennaio 1505 nel Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England, sua padre, John James Weeks, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Lady Alice de Bates, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Watson nel 1517, nel Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 10 figli e 7 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes of Bengeworth, Worcestershire Weeksが1505年1月1日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベンジェウオルスで生まれた時，父John James Weeksは32歳，母Lady Alice de Batesは31歳であった。1517年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベンジェウオルスでThomas Watsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1576年3月にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストラトフォード オン アボンで71歳で死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアストラトフォード オン アボンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes of Bengeworth, Worcestershire Weeks(이)가 1505년 1월 1일 영국 워스스터실르 벤그워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John James Weeks(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Alice de Bates(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1517년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 벤그워스에서 Thomas Watson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1576년 3월 영국 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Agnes of Bengeworth, Worcestershire Weeks nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1505, em Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John James Weeks, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Lady Alice de Bates, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Watson em 1517, em Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em março de 1576, em Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, com 71 anos, e foi sepultada em Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Watson в 1517 г. в местности бенгюорт, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла марта 1576 г. в местности стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия в возрасте 71 год и была похоронена в местности стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия.
